alert.title: Forza Horizon 6 Physics (Japan Festival):
alert.text: AWD center differential traction balances at 70%-85% are key to chassis rotation on mountain passes. All terminology in the manual strictly corresponds to the Spanish festival workshop upgrade tree.

header.title: Community Tunes • Consolidated Report (100+ Videos/Shorts)
header.subtitle: Quantitative audit of Forza Horizon 6 physics, tuning, and secrets obtained from <1>35 meta guides</1>, <2>35 drift shorts</2> and <3>30 secrets guides</3>.

header.title: 🎮 Optimal Logitech G29 Setup — Forza Horizon 6
header.subtitle: Full guide with <1>3 profiles</1> according to your driving style: Racing, Drift, and Off-Road. Always adjust one parameter at a time.

featured.desc: The absolute king of dirt tracks. Built with a lifted rally suspension, optimized AWD differentials (75% central split), and low center of gravity. Click below to inspect its blueprints.

item1.title: Lotus WTAC Time Attack
item1.desc: Achieve a clean lap under 2:10 on Mt. Akina sprint. Setup: R-Class Lotus Exige.

item2.title: Touge Drift King
item2.desc: Accumulate 350,000 drift points in Irohazaka downhill. Setup: Nissan Silvia S15 Spec-R.

item3.title: Kei Car Revival
item3.desc: Win a road racing championship using the Honda Beat (CBR Swap). Setup: C-Class.

videos_meta.intro: The Performance Index (PI) of Forza Horizon 6 implements the <1>R Class (PI 998+)</1> to isolate endurance prototypes and extreme hypercars. The dynamic behavior of RWD vehicles on dry asphalt has been significantly optimized.

rows.notes: Maximum cornering speed and lateral G-force in the game.
Absolute leader in fast zones and asphalt endurance.
Unbeatable cornering and PI balance in winding sections.
Massive launch traction thanks to the electric conversion.

trends.title: Key Meta Trends
trends.i1_text: In C and D classes, Honda CBR1000RR-R and Suzuki Hayabusa engines maximize the power-to-weight ratio of Kei cars without skyrocketing the PI.
trends.i2_text: Heavy, high-power vehicles like the GMC Syclone (with V8) dominate acceleration due to PI gaps in higher gears.

tips.title: Competition Engineering Tips
tips.i1_strong: Use of Sport Transmission in lower classes:
tips.i1_text: Avoid installing racing gearboxes with many gears in D and C classes; save that valuable PI for tires or engine.
tips.i2_text: Softening anti-roll bars on light vehicles allows transverse weight transfer and improves mechanical grip.

cam.title: The New In-Cabin "Drift Cam"
cam.desc: The interior camera dynamically tracks the apex based on the <1>Slip Angle</1>:
cam.i1: Increases immersion by allowing you to look at the exit of mountain corners in real time.
cam.i2: Significantly improves counter-steering control and drift transitions on traditional controllers.

culture.title: Touge & Initial D Culture
culture.desc: The fidelity of the Forza Horizon 6 JDM map (with passes like Mt. Akina) has triggered a wave of anime recreation content:
culture.i1_text: Built mostly with the classic high-revving 4A-GE engine.
culture.i2_text: The noblest RWD drift platform in the game, ideal for tandems.

rows.effect: Maximum front tire contact patch during counter-steer. Prevents unpredictable slides.
Soft to induce controlled body roll and retain inner traction while drifting.
Rear springs 15% softer to settle the rear mass.

glitches.title: Top Speed Glitches (+500 km/h)
glitches.subtitle: Koenigsegg Jesko Express Setup:
glitches.i1_text: Mandatory to ensure linear stability over Tokyo's elevated viaducts.
glitches.i2_text: Manually lengthen the Final Drive to a range between <1>2.20 and 2.45</1>.

easter_eggs.title: Secrets & Easter Eggs
easter_eggs.i1_text: When driving the AE86 in interior view, the cup holder includes a water cup that tilts by inertia but never overflows, emulating the mythical anime physics test.
easter_eggs.i2_text: Driving at moderate speed past "365" convenience stores plays the characteristic electronic welcome chime of real Japanese establishments.

wheel_ghub.title: Logitech G HUB — Configure BEFORE starting the game
wheel_ghub.desc: Mandatory settings in the Logitech software:

rows.notes: 720° → agility balance. 900° → full range for drifting.
ALWAYS disable. Prevents interference with the game's FFB.

wheel_ffb.title: Force Feedback & Steering Feel — Compared Profiles

bug.title: 🔄 Centering Spring Bug
bug.symptom_text: The wheel pulls aggressively to the center when stationary.
bug.solution_text: Disconnect and reconnect the G29 USB cable <1>while sitting in the car in-game</1>. This resets the FFB.

note.title: ⚠️ Important Note about the G29
note.text: The Logitech G29 is a gear-driven wheel. Raising the FFB or Damper excessively causes heat and mechanical rattling noises. Keep moderate values to preserve hardware lifespan.
